name: bytes
description: output the last NUM bytes; or use -c +NUM to output starting with byte NUM of each file --debug indicate which --follow implementation is used

name: follow
description: output appended data as the file grows; an absent option argument means 'descriptor'

name: lines
description: output the last NUM lines, instead of the last 10; or use -n +NUM to skip NUM-1 lines at the start --max-unchanged-stats=N with --follow=name, reopen a FILE which has not changed size after N (default 5) iterations to see if it has been unlinked or renamed (this is the usual case of rotated log files); with inotify, this option is rarely useful --pid=PID with -f, exit after PID no longer exists; can be repeated to watch multiple processes

name: quiet
description: never output headers giving file names --retry keep trying to open a file if it is inaccessible

name: sleep-interval
description: with -f, sleep for approximately N seconds (default 1.0) between iterations; with inotify and --pid=P, check process P at least once every N seconds

name: verbose
description: always output headers giving file names

name: zero-terminated
description: line delimiter is NUL, not newline --help display this help and exit --version output version information and exit
